# Do not edit manually — run 'rulegen generate' to regenerate
# https://github.com/jhoonseo/ai-coding-rules


## Instructions

You are working on rulegen.
CLI tool that generates AI coding rules for every agent from a single config

- Use TypeScript strict mode
- Write JSDoc comments for all public functions
- Keep files under 200 lines
- Use custom error classes for error handling

Do NOT:
- Use 'any' type
- Use synchronous file I/O
- Scan node_modules, .git, or dist directories

## Project Setup

- Language: typescript
- Runtime: node
- Package Manager: npm

### Commands
- Build: `npm run build`
- Test: `npm run test`
- Lint: `npm run lint`

## Code Style

- Use 2 spaces for indentation
- Single quotes
- No semicolons
- Max line length: 100

## Naming Conventions

- Files: kebab-case
- Functions: camelCase
- Constants: UPPER_SNAKE_CASE
- Types: PascalCase

## Project Structure

- src/commands — CLI command handlers (init, generate, sync, doctor)
- src/scanners — Project auto-detection scanners
- src/generators — Agent-specific file generators
- src/types — TypeScript types and Zod schemas
- src/utils — Shared utilities (logger, file, prompt)
- tests — Test files mirroring src/ structure

## Key Patterns

- **async-io**: All file I/O must use async/await
- **custom-errors**: Use custom error classes from utils/errors.ts
- **strategy-pattern**: Generators use strategy pattern via BaseGenerator
